response.justification: The candidate is a strong polyglot generalist with genuine tooling interest and even direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI), but the role's core requirements — deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals, and the Spring ecosystem — are essentially absent from his track record. His recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with Java appearing only peripherally. He would face a steep ramp on the exact skills this position treats as non-negotiable.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience contributing C/C++ components to NI LabWindows/CVI
Proven track record on complex, high-scale systems (low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Demonstrated passion for developer tooling — authored test runners, assertion libraries, and web frameworks as open source
concerns: No substantive Java or Kotlin experience; Java appears only as a minor listed skill
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework exposure
IDE-specific work was brief (~7 months) and dates back to 2015, in C rather than JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
Developer tooling and IDE internals affinity
